mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index indicates expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months when INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05) or the strategy's Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive borrowing costs outweigh growth expectations, depressing corporate investment and equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength spanning both manufacturing and consumer spending, and this joint acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the combined signal underperforms the single-series INDPRO signal by more than 2 annualized percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an elevated consumer price index forces the central bank to maintain or increase restrictive policy, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples, thereby generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average, CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns with directional accuracy below 55% or a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in advance retail sales signals stronger near-term consumer spending, which is the largest component of aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in RSAFS do not coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential construction and downstream demand for durable goods, which reduces aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1500, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months or the median return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market absorption, supporting wage income and household consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a 60-day forward return Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period, or the joint signal is not superior to PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals excess labor market slack, which depresses household income growth and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
